feedback.text: To strengthen the Digital Fairness Act, I propose extending its provisions to include video games and digital media, ensuring publishers plan for end-of-life scenarios. Inspired by the Stop Killing Games campaign, this addition would mandate that publishers provide offline modes, server software, or other solutions to preserve access to purchased games and media after online support ceases. This protects consumers rights to enjoy their digital purchases indefinitely, promotes sustainable digital preservation, and prevents the loss of cultural and entertainment value due to server shutdowns or platform obsolescence.
